title: El Salvador's estado de excepción marks 4 years — 49th extension approved
summary: El Salvador's state of exception reached its 4-year anniversary (March 27, 2022–2026) with its 49th consecutive legislative extension. More than 91,650 people have been detained; human rights groups document at least 504 deaths in custody, 6,889 human rights complaints, and 98% of cases involving arbitrary detention. FAES military support for police extended through December 31, 2026.

summary: 215 Kenyan police officers withdrew from Haiti on March 17 as the Kenya-led Multinational Security Support Mission (MSS) began transitioning to the UN-mandated Gang Suppression Force (GSF). The GSF, authorized by UNSC Resolution 2793 (Oct 2025), will eventually field 5,500 personnel from Chad and other troop-contributing countries arriving summer 2026.

mission_desc: Organized crime network interaction with security forces
title: CJNG leader 'El Mencho' killed in Jalisco military operation
summary: Mexican Army, with US intelligence support, killed Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes ('El Mencho'), head of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, in a special operations strike in Tapalpa, Jalisco. Widespread CJNG retaliatory violence followed across Jalisco state.

mission_desc: Receipt of US/foreign military assistance or arms transfers
title: US approves $1.5B in FMS to Peru for Callao naval base modernization
summary: The State Department approved a $1.5 billion Foreign Military Sale to Peru to upgrade and relocate the Callao naval base — the largest US-Peru security cooperation package in recent history. The sale reflects SOUTHCOM's priority on deepening Pacific-facing naval partnerships amid Chinese port investment in the region.

mission_desc: Removal of officers for political/loyalty reasons
title: FANB issues loyalty statement backing Rodríguez; DGCIM detains at least 12 officers suspected of cooperation with US
summary: Defense Minister Vladimir Padrino López issued a statement pledging FANB loyalty to constitutional continuity under Rodríguez. DGCIM simultaneously detained at least 12 mid- to senior-level officers on suspicion of having facilitated or failed to prevent the January 3 operation. The scale of detentions suggests deep uncertainty about institutional loyalty within the armed forces. Unconfirmed reports indicate CEOFANB General Hernández Lárez tendered his resignation but was not accepted.

mission_desc: Attempted or successful seizure of political power by military
title: Maduro arraigned in New York on narco-terrorism charges; Delcy Rodríguez sworn in as acting president
summary: Nicolás Maduro and Cilia Flores were transported to New York and arraigned on January 5, 2026, on charges of narco-terrorism conspiracy, cocaine importation, and possession of machine guns. A federal judge ordered Maduro detained. Simultaneously, Venezuela's Supreme Tribunal of Justice ordered Vice President Delcy Rodríguez to assume the interim presidency. Under the Venezuelan Constitution, she may hold the position for up to six months before elections must be called.

mission_desc: Attempted or successful seizure of political power by military
title: Operation Absolute Resolve: US forces conduct strike on Caracas, capture President Maduro
summary: Beginning at approximately 2:00 AM local time, US special operations forces and airstrikes targeted Maduro's compound and air defence infrastructure in Caracas, La Guaira, and Higuerote. Delta Force units apprehended Nicolás Maduro and Cilia Flores. At least 23 killed during the operation. The action — formally unilateral — was justified by the Trump administration under the 2020 narco-terrorism indictment. The UN Security Council emergency session condemned the operation; Russia and China vetoed a binding resolution.
